source.definition: to give someone facts or knowledge about something

explanation.sourceText: The English word "inform" comes from the Latin verb "informare," meaning "to shape" or "to give form or idea."
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „inform” provine din verbul latin „informare”, care înseamnă „a da formă” sau „a da o idee”.

source.additional: Regular verb. Used to convey knowledge or news.

target.definition: a da informații cu privire la ceva

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian verb "a informa" was borrowed in the 19th century from the French "informer," which in turn derives from the Latin "informare."
explanation.targetText: Verbul românesc „a informa” a fost împrumutat în secolul al XIX-lea din franceză, „informer”, care la rândul său provine din latină, „informare”.

target.additional: Regular verb of the 1st conjugation (-a). Commonly used in both formal and informal contexts.

examples.sourceText: The explanation was clear.
I have good news.
I inform my friends about the event.
examples.targetText: Eu informez prietenii mei despre eveniment.

examples.sourceText: Do you inform me about the latest news?
examples.targetText: Mă informezi despre cele mai recente știri?

examples.sourceText: He informs the participants about the program.
We inform everyone in time.
examples.targetText: Noi informăm pe toată lumea la timp.

examples.sourceText: Do you inform the others about the changes?
They inform the group about the progress.
I have already informed you.
They have informed the participants.
He informed the viewers about the topic.
I had informed you before you arrived.
I will inform the data as soon as possible.
examples.targetText: Voi informa datele cât mai curând posibil.

examples.sourceText: He will inform the results later.
examples.targetText: El o să informeze rezultatele mai târziu.

examples.sourceText: I would inform you if I could.
It is important that you also inform the others.
examples.targetText: Este important să-i informezi și pe ceilalți.

examples.sourceText: Inform your colleagues!
Please inform all participants!
The informed persons know.
The data is now informed.
While I informed the group, I asked many questions.
examples.targetText: Informând grupul, am pus multe întrebări.

learningTips.text: The English word "inform" and the Romanian "a informa" are cognates, making it easier to remember.
Imagine you are informing a group of friends about a new movie—this helps link the word to sharing news.
Remember the root "form" in "inform" relates to shaping or giving an idea, which connects to giving information.
Practice sentences where you inform someone, like "I inform my colleagues," to solidify usage.
